# US Stock Watcher Daily Comprehensive Brief — 2026-07-15

**Moderate / selective risk-on · composite +22 · confidence 41%**

*Data as of: 2026-07-15T15:03:31 · data quality: fresh*

## Executive Summary

The overall market-state score stands at +22 out of 100, placing the current regime in Moderate / selective risk-on territory, with confidence at 41% based on four measured components. Six inputs — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning — remain unmeasured and have been reweighted out of the composite for this reading.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Equity benchmarks are firmer across the board today. The S&P 500 trades at 7,557.45, up 0.56% on the day, while the Nasdaq-100 sits at 29,393.24, up 0.44%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average has advanced to 52,694.61 (+0.37%), and small caps are outperforming, with the Russell 2000 up 0.88% to 2,979.17.

**Market drivers.** Four forces are currently shaping the tape. Volatility is acting as a supportive influence, as is the prevailing index trend, and the yield curve is likewise contributing constructively. The one headwind in the mix is the US dollar, which is working against the broader risk-on impulse.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +22/100 continues to characterize conditions as Moderate / selective risk-on, with confidence held at 41% on the strength of four measured components. As noted above, Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning remain unmeasured and are excluded via reweighting.

**Sector & rotation.** Leadership today is concentrated in Financials, Utilities, and Health Care, while Consumer Staples, Technology, and Energy are lagging the broader market.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.62%. The dollar index (DXY) has slipped to 100.75, down 0.52% on the day. In commodities, WTI crude is off 0.77% at $78.73, while gold is essentially flat, edging up 0.06% to $4,063.50.

## Outlook

**Short term.** The near-term bias is constructive, consistent with the prevailing "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 41% confidence reading. This view would flip should the overall composite score cross below zero.

**Medium term.** The medium-term stance likewise remains constructive under the same "Moderate / selective risk-on" classification and 41% confidence level, with the same zero-line threshold serving as the trigger for a reassessment.

**Medium-to-long term.** Over the medium-to-long horizon, the constructive bias persists on the same basis — the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state at 41% confidence — and would similarly reverse if the composite score moves negative.
